name: 35th & Indiana Currency Exchange
description_short: Multi-service currency exchange in Chicago offering check cashing, money transfers, bill pay, and government services like vehicle registration and tax preparation.
description_long:
New 35th & Indiana Currency Exchange, Inc. is a brick-and-mortar financial services provider located at 126 E 35th St. in Chicago, Illinois. Operating since at least the time of Currency Exchange Association membership, the company positions itself as an alternative to traditional banks and corporate financial institutions. The business is open 7 days a week, with extended hours Monday–Friday (7 AM–8 PM) and reduced hours on weekends (8 AM–6 PM Saturday, 9 AM–5 PM Sunday).

The company offers a broad suite of financial and government services rather than specializing in a single product. Core offerings include check cashing (personal and business), money orders, money transfers, bill payment, ATM access, and foreign currency exchange. They also provide government-related services such as vehicle title and registration handling, Cook County vital records retrieval, license plate renewals, and city vehicle sticker registration. Additional services span tax preparation, notary services, prepaid cards, coin counting, private mailboxes, and even Bitcoin access.

The company differentiates itself by consolidating multiple financial and administrative needs under one roof, marketing this convenience explicitly: "Sure, you could go to multiple locations to handle all of your financial needs and get lost in the shuffle of everyone else while you're at it. Or, you could just do it all in one place." Their messaging emphasizes personal service and treating customers as individuals rather than account numbers, positioning themselves against impersonal corporate banking.

As a check-cashing and currency exchange service, this business is best suited for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate cash access, government services, or international money transfer rather than credit building, borrowing, or investment products. The breadth of services makes them a practical errand destination, but customers should confirm specific service availability and fee structures before visiting, as the website provides limited pricing transparency.
pros: One-stop-shop for 30+ financial and government services including check cashing, vehicle registration, tax prep, and vital records
Extended weekday hours (7 AM–8 PM) and weekend availability (including Sundays 9 AM–5 PM)
Instant processing for vehicle registration with direct state facility integration—"in and out with your actual Illinois license plates"
Foreign currency exchange service for international transfers and travel needs
ATM access available on-site for convenient cash withdrawal and balance inquiries
Handles both personal and business check cashing without requiring a bank account
Accessible by phone (312-225-6722) for service confirmation before visiting
cons: No pricing or fee information disclosed on website—customers must call or visit to learn costs for check cashing, money orders, or other services
Check cashing and currency exchange services typically carry higher fees than traditional banks, but no comparison provided
Limited online functionality—requires in-person visits for most services; no digital or mobile options mentioned
As a check-cashing service, does not offer credit products, savings accounts, or wealth-building tools
Website lacks transparency on which services require advance notice or specific documentation, creating potential for wasted trips
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers in Chicago needing immediate check cashing or cash access without a bank account
Chicago residents handling vehicle registration, title transfers, or renewal stickers who want one-location convenience
International remitters and travelers needing foreign currency exchange or money transfer services
Small business owners requiring business check cashing services alongside other financial needs
diagnosis: New 35th & Indiana Currency Exchange is best for Chicago residents who need check cashing, government services, or international transfers in a single location without a bank account. The main caveat is the complete lack of fee transparency on their website—customers must call or visit to learn actual costs, and check-cashing services typically charge higher fees than banks.
